title: Epic: Tree Biodiversity Upload Wizard
description:
## Why
Community members need to upload tree biodiversity data (CSV or KoboToolbox exports) into the ATProto PDS. This is the primary data ingestion path for field-collected tree measurements.

## What Success Looks Like
- DwC occurrence and measurement mutations exist in mutations-core with full Effect error handling
- mutations-next exposes these via tRPC
- bumicerts has a /upload/trees route with a 4-step wizard: File Drop -> Column Mapping -> Preview & Validate -> Upload to PDS
- The wizard auto-detects column mappings for both generic CSV and KoboToolbox formats
- Valid rows are written as app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence + app.gainforest.dwc.measurement records
- Invalid rows show per-field validation errors; only valid rows are uploaded
- Auth gate at Step 4 preserves wizard state across OAuth redirects

## Architecture
- mutations-core: dwc.occurrence.create, dwc.measurement.create (Effect programs)
- mutations-next: tRPC router wiring
- bumicerts/lib/upload/: pure logic (types, schemas, column-mapper, kobo-mapper)
- bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/: 4-step wizard UI

## Key Constraints
- Follow the exact Effect mutation pattern (tagged errors, dollar-parse validation, AtprotoAgent service)
- Use tRPC mutations (not raw server actions) for PDS writes
- Use shadcn/ui components for the wizard UI
- Occurrence records use putRecord with explicit TID rkeys; measurements link via occurrenceRef
- KoboToolbox GPS field splitting must handle combined lat/lon/alt/accuracy format
- SKIP_SENTINEL pattern for Radix Select (not empty string)

title: Add DwC occurrence create mutation to mutations-core
description:
## Files
- packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/create.ts (create)
- packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/utils/types.ts (create)
- packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/utils/errors.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a DwC occurrence create mutation following the EXACT pattern of the audio recording mutation at packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/organization.recordings.audio/create.ts.

### Collection: app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence
### Key type: tid (PDS assigns when rkey omitted)

### CreateDwcOccurrenceInput type:
```ts
type CreateDwcOccurrenceInput = {
  scientificName: string;
  eventDate: string;
  decimalLatitude: string;  // stored as string in ATProto
  decimalLongitude: string; // stored as string in ATProto
  basisOfRecord?: string;   // defaults to 'HumanObservation'
  vernacularName?: string;
  recordedBy?: string;
  locality?: string;
  country?: string;
  countryCode?: string;
  occurrenceRemarks?: string;
  habitat?: string;
  samplingProtocol?: string;
  kingdom?: string;         // defaults to 'Plantae'
  occurrenceID?: string;    // UUID, auto-generated if omitted
  occurrenceStatus?: string; // defaults to 'present'
  geodeticDatum?: string;   // defaults to 'EPSG:4326'
  license?: string;         // defaults to 'CC-BY-4.0'
  projectRef?: string;      // AT-URI, optional
  rkey?: string;            // optional, PDS assigns TID if omitted
};
```

### Mutation flow (simpler than audio — no blobs):
1. Build candidate record with $type = COLLECTION, add createdAt, apply defaults
2. Validate with $parse from generated types
3. Write with createRecord (rkey optional)
4. Return { uri, cid, rkey, record }

### Error classes:
- DwcOccurrenceValidationError extends Data.TaggedError('DwcOccurrenceValidationError')
- DwcOccurrencePdsError extends Data.TaggedError('DwcOccurrencePdsError')

### Imports:
- $parse from '@gainforest/generated/app/gainforest/dwc/occurrence.defs'
- AtprotoAgent from '../../services/AtprotoAgent'
- createRecord from '../../utils/shared'
- Use crypto.randomUUID() for occurrenceID default

## Don't
- Don't add blob handling (occurrence records have no blobs)
- Don't create update/upsert/delete yet (separate tasks)
- Don't modify namespace.ts or index.ts (separate task)
- Don't add tags field to the record (it exists in the lexicon but is not part of this input)
acceptance_criteria:
1. packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/create.ts exists and exports createDwcOccurrence
2. packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/utils/types.ts exports CreateDwcOccurrenceInput, DwcOccurrenceMutationResult, DwcOccurrenceRecord
3. packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/utils/errors.ts exports DwcOccurrenceValidationError, DwcOccurrencePdsError (both Data.TaggedError)
4. createDwcOccurrence follows the Effect pattern: returns Effect.Effect<DwcOccurrenceMutationResult, DwcOccurrenceValidationError | DwcOccurrencePdsError, AtprotoAgent>
5. Uses $parse from @gainforest/generated/app/gainforest/dwc/occurrence.defs for validation
6. Uses createRecord from utils/shared (not putRecord) — PDS assigns TID when rkey is omitted
7. bun run build succeeds from the monorepo root
close_reason: 4011811 Add DwC occurrence create mutation to mutations-core

title: Create PreviewStep component
description:
## Files
- apps/bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/_components/PreviewStep.tsx (create)

## What to do
Create Step 3 of the wizard — data preview and validation. Reference: C:\Users\infec\MyFolders\Documents\Work\Gainforest\green_globe\src\app\upload\_components\PreviewStep.tsx

### Props
```ts
type PreviewStepProps = {
  parsedData: Record<string, string>[];
  mappings: ColumnMapping[];
  onBack: () => void;
  onNext: (validRows: ValidatedRow[]) => void;
};
```

### Behavior
1. On mount (useMemo): run applyMappings(parsedData, mappings) then parseAndValidateRows(mappedRows)
2. Display summary: N total rows, N valid, N errors
3. Table showing first 20 rows with columns: Row #, Status (icon), Key fields (scientificName, eventDate, lat, lon)
4. Valid rows: green CheckCircle icon
5. Error rows: red XCircle icon, clickable to expand error detail
6. Expanded error detail shows: field path + error message for each Zod issue
7. Collapsible Error Summary section at top showing aggregated error counts per field
8. Footer: Back button + 'Upload N Valid Rows' button (disabled if 0 valid rows)
9. onNext(validationResult.valid) passes only the valid rows to Step 4

### Error summary builder:
Count how many rows have errors on each field. Display: 'scientificName: 5 rows, decimalLatitude: 3 rows'

## Don't
- Don't handle PDS writes (that's Step 4)
- Don't handle auth (that's Step 4)
- Don't call tRPC mutations
- Don't compute applyMappings twice (single useMemo)
acceptance_criteria:
1. apps/bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/_components/PreviewStep.tsx exists
2. Runs applyMappings + parseAndValidateRows on mount
3. Shows first 20 rows in a table with per-row validation status (green check / red X)
4. Error rows are expandable showing per-field error messages
5. Shows error summary with counts per field
6. Upload N Valid Rows button passes only valid rows to onNext
7. bun run bumicerts:lint passes

title: Create Zod validation schemas for tree upload
description:
## Files
- apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/schemas.ts (create)

## What to do
Create Zod validation schemas for the tree upload wizard. Reference: C:\Users\infec\MyFolders\Documents\Work\Gainforest\green_globe\src\lib\upload\schemas.ts

### OccurrenceRowSchema
- scientificName: z.string().min(1)
- eventDate: z.string() with .refine() validating 5 regex patterns
- decimalLatitude: z.coerce.number().min(-90).max(90)
- decimalLongitude: z.coerce.number().min(-180).max(180)
- basisOfRecord: z.string().default('HumanObservation')
- Optional strings: vernacularName, recordedBy, locality, country, countryCode, occurrenceRemarks, habitat, samplingProtocol, kingdom

### MeasurementFields (merged into TreeRowSchema)
- height: z.coerce.number().positive().optional()
- dbh: z.coerce.number().positive().optional()
- diameter: z.coerce.number().positive().optional()
- canopyCover: z.coerce.number().min(0).max(100).optional()

### parseAndValidateRows(rows: Record<string, string>[]): ValidationResult
1. For each row, run TreeRowSchema.safeParse(row)
2. If success: extract occurrence fields + measurement fields into ValidatedRow
3. extractFloraMeasurement returns null if NO measurement fields have values
4. If failure: map Zod issues to { path, message } format into RowError

### Helper functions:
- extractOccurrence(parsed): OccurrenceInput — picks occurrence fields from parsed data
- extractFloraMeasurement(parsed): FloraMeasurementBundle | null — picks measurement fields, returns null if all undefined

## Don't
- Don't import React
- Don't use any framework dependencies (only zod)
- Don't import papaparse
acceptance_criteria:
1. apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/schemas.ts exists
2. Exports OccurrenceRowSchema, TreeRowSchema, parseAndValidateRows
3. parseAndValidateRows returns { valid: ValidatedRow[], errors: RowError[] }
4. Required fields: scientificName, eventDate, decimalLatitude (-90..90), decimalLongitude (-180..180)
5. Measurement fields use z.coerce.number().positive().optional()
6. eventDate accepts 5 formats: YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, YYYY, ISO datetime
7. floraMeasurement is null when no measurement fields present (not empty object)
8. bun run bumicerts:lint passes
close_reason: a4bba11 Create Zod validation schemas for tree upload

title: Create CSV column mapper with auto-detection
description:
## Files
- apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/column-mapper.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a generic CSV column auto-detection and mapping engine. Reference: C:\Users\infec\MyFolders\Documents\Work\Gainforest\green_globe\src\lib\upload\column-mapper.ts

### autoDetectMappings(headers: string[]): ColumnMapping[]
Pattern-based auto-detection. 13 pattern groups:
- lat/latitude/decimal_latitude/decimallatitude/y -> decimalLatitude
- lon/lng/longitude/decimal_longitude/decimallongitude/x -> decimalLongitude
- species/scientific_name/scientificname/taxon/taxon_name -> scientificName
- date/event_date/eventdate/observation_date/collection_date -> eventDate
- recorder/recorded_by/recordedby/observer/collector -> recordedBy
- common_name/commonname/vernacular/vernacular_name/vernacularname -> vernacularName
- remarks/notes/occurrence_remarks/occurrenceremarks/comments -> occurrenceRemarks
- country -> country
- locality/location/site/place -> locality
- habitat/habitat_type -> habitat
- dbh/diameter_breast_height/trunk_diameter -> dbh
- height/tree_height/total_height -> height
- crown_diameter/canopy_diameter/canopy_cover -> canopyCover
Algorithm: normalize header to lowercase, iterate KNOWN_PATTERNS, first match wins, use claimedTargets Set to prevent duplicates. Important: 'name' alone must NOT map to vernacularName (regression from green_globe).

### applyMappings(rows, mappings): MappedRow[]
For each row: iterate mappings, look up sourceColumn in row, apply transform if present, set targetField in output. Skip empty/undefined values.

## Don't
- Don't handle KoboToolbox format (separate task)
- Don't add React imports
- Don't add zod dependency
acceptance_criteria:
1. apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/column-mapper.ts exists
2. Exports autoDetectMappings(headers: string[]): ColumnMapping[]
3. Exports applyMappings(rows: Record<string, string>[], mappings: ColumnMapping[]): MappedRow[]
4. autoDetectMappings handles 13+ known patterns (lat, lon, species, date, etc.)
5. Case-insensitive matching, first-match-wins per target field
6. applyMappings supports transform functions and drops empty values
7. bun run bumicerts:lint passes
close_reason: c4a653f Create CSV column mapper with auto-detection

title: Create KoboToolbox format detector
description:
## Files
- apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/kobo-mapper.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a KoboToolbox CSV format detector with GPS field splitting. Reference: C:\Users\infec\MyFolders\Documents\Work\Gainforest\green_globe\src\lib\upload\kobo-mapper.ts

### detectKoboFormat(headers: string[]): { isKobo, confidence, mappings }

KoboToolbox-specific patterns (koboSpecific: true):
- gps, geopoint -> combined GPS field (lat/lon split)
- _gps_latitude, gps_latitude -> decimalLatitude
- _gps_longitude, gps_longitude -> decimalLongitude
- plant_name -> scientificName
- fcd-tree_records-tree_time -> eventDate
- _submission_time, submission_time -> eventDate

Generic patterns (koboSpecific: false):
- species, scientific_name -> scientificName
- date, event_date -> eventDate
- latitude, lat -> decimalLatitude
- longitude, lon -> decimalLongitude
- dbh -> dbh
- height, tree_height -> height
- habitat -> habitat
- remarks, notes -> occurrenceRemarks

### GPS Combined Field Splitting
When a header matches 'gps' or 'geopoint', produce TWO mappings:
```ts
{ sourceColumn: header, targetField: 'decimalLatitude', transform: (v) => v.trim().split(/\s+/)[0] ?? '' }
{ sourceColumn: header, targetField: 'decimalLongitude', transform: (v) => v.trim().split(/\s+/)[1] ?? '' }
```

### Detection logic:
- confidence = matchedHeaders / totalHeaders
- strongMatchCount = count of koboSpecific matches
- isKobo = confidence >= 0.3 AND strongMatchCount >= 1

### Matching: case-insensitive, exact match first, then substring (includes)

## Don't
- Don't import React
- Don't add framework dependencies
- Don't handle non-GPS Kobo metadata fields (keep focused on tree data)
acceptance_criteria:
1. apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/kobo-mapper.ts exists
2. Exports detectKoboFormat(headers: string[]): { isKobo: boolean; confidence: number; mappings: ColumnMapping[] }
3. isKobo requires confidence >= 0.3 AND at least 1 koboSpecific pattern match
4. GPS combined fields (lat lon alt accuracy) produce TWO ColumnMapping entries with transform functions
5. Standard DwC headers alone do NOT trigger isKobo = true (false positive guard)
6. bun run bumicerts:lint passes

title: Create CSV parser hook and FileDropStep component
description:
## Files
- apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/use-csv-parser.ts (create)
- apps/bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/_components/FileDropStep.tsx (create)

## What to do

### use-csv-parser.ts
A 'use client' React hook wrapping PapaParse:
```ts
export function useCsvParser() {
  // State: parsedData, headers, rowCount, error, isParsing
  // Methods: parseFile(file: File), reset()
  // Config: header: true, skipEmptyLines: true, dynamicTyping: false, encoding: 'UTF-8'
}
```
Add papaparse as a dependency: cd apps/bumicerts && bun add papaparse && bun add -d @types/papaparse

### FileDropStep.tsx
A 'use client' component for Step 1 of the wizard.

Props:
```ts
type FileDropStepProps = {
  onFileAndMappings: (
    file: File,
    parsedData: Record<string, string>[],
    headers: string[],
    mappings: ColumnMapping[]
  ) => void;
};
```

Behavior:
1. Drag-and-drop zone OR click to select file
2. Accept .csv, .tsv files only, max 10MB
3. On file selected: call parseFile from useCsvParser hook
4. On parse complete: run detectKoboFormat(headers). If isKobo, use those mappings. Else run autoDetectMappings(headers)
5. Show file info: name, size, row count, detected format (Kobo/Generic)
6. Continue button calls onFileAndMappings with all data

UI: Use shadcn Card, Button. Use lucide-react icons (Upload, FileSpreadsheet). Tailwind dark mode classes.

## Don't
- Don't handle column mapping (that's Step 2)
- Don't handle validation (that's Step 3)
- Don't implement auth (that's Step 4)
acceptance_criteria:
1. apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/use-csv-parser.ts exists with useCsvParser hook
2. apps/bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/_components/FileDropStep.tsx exists
3. FileDropStep accepts onFileAndMappings callback prop
4. Supports CSV and TSV file drop/click-to-upload (max 10MB)
5. Runs detectKoboFormat first; falls back to autoDetectMappings
6. Shows file name, row count, and detected format after parsing
7. papaparse dependency is added to bumicerts package.json
8. bun run bumicerts:lint passes
close_reason: 7e5e8da Create CSV parser hook and FileDropStep component

title: Create ColumnMappingStep component
description:
## Files
- apps/bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/_components/ColumnMappingStep.tsx (create)

## What to do
Create Step 2 of the wizard — column mapping UI. Reference: C:\Users\infec\MyFolders\Documents\Work\Gainforest\green_globe\src\app\upload\_components\ColumnMappingStep.tsx

### Props
```ts
type ColumnMappingStepProps = {
  headers: string[];
  mappings: ColumnMapping[];
  sampleData?: Record<string, string>[];  // first few rows for preview
  onMappingsChange: (mappings: ColumnMapping[]) => void;
  onBack: () => void;
  onNext: () => void;
};
```

### Behavior
1. Display each source column as a row with: column name, sample value (first row), dropdown to select target field
2. Pre-populate dropdowns from initial mappings (auto-detected in Step 1)
3. Dropdown uses shadcn Select with SelectGroup/SelectLabel for categories
4. CRITICAL: Use SKIP_SENTINEL = '__skip__' as the value for 'Skip this column'. Radix Select crashes on empty string value
5. Group target fields by category from TARGET_FIELDS: 'Required', 'Optional', 'Measurements'
6. Required field indicator: show red asterisk or badge for required fields
7. Validate: enable Continue button only when all 4 required fields (scientificName, eventDate, decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude) are mapped
8. Show warning badge when two source columns map to the same target field

### When user changes a mapping:
- Update the ColumnMapping array
- Remove transform function when user manually changes mapping (transforms come from auto-detect only)

## Don't
- Don't run validation (that's Step 3)
- Don't call applyMappings here (that's Step 3)
- Don't handle file parsing (that's Step 1)
acceptance_criteria:
1. apps/bumicerts/app/(upload)/upload/trees/_components/ColumnMappingStep.tsx exists
2. Shows all source columns with dropdown to map to target fields
3. Uses SKIP_SENTINEL = '__skip__' for skip option (not empty string)
4. Grouped dropdown: Required Occurrence / Optional Occurrence / Measurements
5. All 4 required fields must be mapped for Continue button to be enabled
6. Shows warnings for duplicate target mappings
7. bun run bumicerts:lint passes

title: Fix: column-mapper.ts was not delivered in task 1.6 commit (from atproto-packages-1.6)
description: Review of atproto-packages-1.6 found: The task was closed with commit c4a653f ('Create CSV column mapper with auto-detection') but that commit contains ZERO file changes. The actual column-mapper.ts was created in commit 347b9bb (task atproto-packages-1.11 'Create UploadStep component with tRPC integration'). Evidence: git log --all --oneline --diff-filter=A -- apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/column-mapper.ts returns '347b9bb Create UploadStep component with tRPC integration (atproto-packages-1.11)'. The 1.6 close_reason commit c4a653f shows no files in --name-only output. The file exists and is correct, but it was bundled into the wrong task's commit, making the audit trail misleading and the 1.6 close invalid.

title: Fix: schemas.ts was delivered in task 1.3 commit instead of task 1.5 (from atproto-packages-1.5)
description: Review of atproto-packages-1.5 found: The task was closed with commit a4bba11 ('Wire DwC mutations into namespace, index exports, and tRPC router') but that commit also created apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/schemas.ts — a file that belongs to task 1.5 ('Create Zod validation schemas for tree upload'). Evidence: git log --all --oneline --diff-filter=A -- apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/schemas.ts returns 'a4bba11 Wire DwC mutations into namespace, index exports, and tRPC router (atproto-packages-1.3)'. Task 1.3 was only supposed to touch packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/namespace.ts, packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/index.ts, and packages/atproto-mutations-next/src/trpc/router.ts. The schemas.ts file is correct and present, but it was bundled into the wrong task's commit. Task 1.5 close_reason points to commit a4bba11 which is actually the 1.3 commit, making the audit trail for both tasks incorrect.

title: Fix: OccurrenceInput lat/lon typed as number but CreateDwcOccurrenceInput expects string — implicit coercion at bumicerts→mutations-core seam (from atproto-packages-1.1)
description: Review of the integration seam between bumicerts and mutations-core found a type mismatch: apps/bumicerts/lib/upload/types.ts defines OccurrenceInput.decimalLatitude and OccurrenceInput.decimalLongitude as 'number', but packages/atproto-mutations-core/src/mutations/dwc.occurrence/utils/types.ts defines CreateDwcOccurrenceInput.decimalLatitude and CreateDwcOccurrenceInput.decimalLongitude as 'string'. The UploadStep.tsx works around this with explicit String() coercion (lines 113-114), but the type boundary is leaky: any future caller that passes OccurrenceInput directly to createDwcOccurrence will get a TypeScript type error (or silent runtime coercion if types are widened). The spec for task 1.1 says 'decimalLatitude: string — stored as string in ATProto' and the spec for task 1.4 says 'decimalLatitude: number' in OccurrenceInput. These two specs are in conflict. The seam should either: (a) change OccurrenceInput.decimalLatitude/decimalLongitude to string to match the PDS type, or (b) add an explicit adapter/mapper function that converts OccurrenceInput to CreateDwcOccurrenceInput. Evidence: types.ts line 12-13 (number), dwc.occurrence/utils/types.ts line 16-17 (string), UploadStep.tsx lines 113-114 (String() coercion).
